name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayah

```arabic
ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ جَاعِلِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ رُسُلًا أُو۟لِىٓ أَجْنِحَةٍ مَّثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ ۚ يَزِيدُ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (١)
```

```english
All praise belongs to Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth, maker of the angels [His] messengers, possessing wings, two, three or four [of them]. He adds to the creation whatever He wishes. Indeed Allah has power over all things. (1)
```

## (Commentary)

The purpose of the Surah is to expound the three principles: His Oneness, the Exalted, in His Lordship (الربوبية); the messengership of the Messenger; and the Return (المعاد) — *the resurrection and final gathering of souls* — to Him, as well as establishing the proof for these. To achieve this, it employs a series of His magnificent blessings, both celestial and terrestrial, and points toward His precise governance (تدبيره) of the affairs of the world in general and man in particular.

Prior to this detailed exposition, the Surah presents a summary indication that the opening and withholding of mercy (الرحمة) — which is the effusion of blessing (إفاضة النعمة) and its cessation — is restricted to Him, the Exalted, in His saying: **ما يَفْتَحِ اللهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلا مُمْسِكَ لَها** "Whatever mercy Allah unfolds for the people, no one can withhold it" (35:2).

Preceding even that is an indication of the intermediaries (وسائط) of this unfolded mercy and bestowed blessings, namely the angels (الملائكة) who mediate between Him, the Exalted, and His creation in carrying various types of blessings from Him and delivering them to His creation. Thus, He opened the Surah by mentioning them.

The Surah is Meccan (مكية), as indicated by the context (سياق) of its verses. Some have excepted two verses, namely His saying: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتابَ اللهِ** "Indeed those who recite the Book of Allah..." (35:29) and His saying: **ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا** "Then We caused those whom We chose... to inherit the Book" (35:32), but this is not apparent from the context of the two verses.

His saying, the Exalted: **الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ** "All praise belongs to Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth" (35:1). Originating (الفطر) — according to what al-Raghib mentioned — is the longitudinal splitting. Applying the term "Originator" (الفاطر) to Him, the Exalted, is by way of a metaphorical consideration (عناية استعارية), as if He split non-existence (العدم) and brought forth from its belly the heavens and the earth. The sum of its meaning is that He is the bringer of the heavens and the earth into existence through an initial creation (إيجادا ابتدائيا) without a prior model. Thus, its meaning is close to the meaning of the "Inimitable Originator" (البديع) and the "Innovator" (المبدع). The difference between innovation (الإبداع) and origination (الفطر) is that the consideration in innovation relates to the negation of a prior model, while in origination, it relates to the repelling of non-existence and bringing the thing into existence from the start — unlike a craftsman who assembles different materials to manifest a new form that did not exist.

What is meant by "the heavens and the earth" is the totality of the witnessed world (العالم المشهود); thus it encompasses them and whatever creatures are within them. This would be of the category of mentioning the major parts while intending the whole metaphorically. Alternatively, it refers to the heavens and the earth themselves, out of regard for their status due to the greatness of their creation and the wonder of their affair, as He said: **لَخَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ خَلْقِ النَّاسِ** "Surely the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of mankind" (40:57).

In any case, His saying: **فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ** is among His names, the Exalted, used as an attribute for Allah. What is intended by the description is continuity (الاستمرار) rather than the past only, because bringing into existence is continuous and the effusion of existence (فيض الوجود) is uninterrupted; were it to be interrupted, all things would cease to exist.

The presentation of one description after another is to signal the reasons for the restriction of praise (الحمد) to Him, the Exalted. It is as if it were said: Praise belongs to Allah for His originating the heavens and the earth, and for His making the angels messengers possessing wings. Thus, He, the Exalted, is praised, for He has brought forth nothing but the beautiful.

His saying, the Exalted: **جاعِلِ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ** "maker of the angels [His] messengers, possessing wings, two, three, and four [pairs]" (35:1). The angels (الملائكة) is the plural of *malak* (ملك). They are beings whom Allah created and made as intermediaries between Himself and the witnessed world. He has entrusted them with the ontological (التكوينية) and legislative (التشريعية) affairs of the world; they are honored servants who do not disobey Allah in what He commands them and they do what they are commanded.

So His saying, the Exalted: **جاعِلِ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً** "maker of the angels [His] messengers" suggests, or rather indicates, that all angels — as "the angels" (الملائكة) is a plural defined by the definite article implying generality — are messengers and intermediaries between Him and His creation in the execution of His ontological and legislative commands.

There is no reason to restrict "messengers" in the verse to the angels who descend upon the prophets (peace be upon them). The Quran has applied the term "messengers" to other angels, such as His saying, the Exalted: **حَتَّى إِذا جاءَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنا** "until when death comes to one of you, Our messengers take him away" (6:61), and His saying: **إِنَّ رُسُلَنا يَكْتُبُونَ ما تَمْكُرُونَ** "Indeed Our messengers write down what you plot" (10:21), and His saying: **وَلَمَّا جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا إِبْراهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرى قالُوا إِنَّا مُهْلِكُوا أَهْلِ هذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ** "And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news, they said, ‘We are indeed going to destroy the people of this town’" (29:31).

Wings (الأجنحة) is the plural of *janah* (جناح). In a bird, it is like the hand in a human; it is used as a means to ascend into the air, descend from it, and move from place to place by flying.

Thus, the existence of the angel is equipped with that by which it performs something analogous to what a bird performs with its wing. It moves by it from the heaven to the earth by the command of Allah, and ascends by it from the earth to the heaven, and from any place to any place. The Quran has named it a "wing" (جناحا), and this necessitates nothing more than the realization of the intended purpose of a wing. As for it being of the same nature as the wings of most birds, possessing feathers and down, the mere application of the word does not necessitate this, just as it did not necessitate it in similar instances like the terms Throne (العرش), Chair (الكرسي), Tablet (اللوح), Pen (القلم), and others.

His saying: **أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ** "possessing wings, two, three, and four [pairs]" is an attribute of the angels. *Mathna* (مثنى), *thulatha* (ثلاث), and *ruba‘a* (رباع) are words indicating repeated numbers, i.e., two by two, three by three, and four by four. It is as if it were said: He made the angels, some possessing two wings, some possessing three wings, and some possessing four wings.

His saying: **يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ** "He adds to creation whatever He wishes" (35:1) is not devoid of a suggestion, according to the context, that among them are those whose wings exceed four.

His saying: **إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ** "Indeed Allah has power over all things" (35:1) is a justification (تعليل) for all that preceded it, or for the final sentence, though the former is more apparent.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Bihar*, from *al-Ikhtisas*, with its chain of transmission from al-Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad, reaching back to Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: **إن الله عز وجل خلق الملائكة من نور** — "Indeed Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created the angels from light..." (the narration).

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: **خلق الله الملائكة مختلفة وقد أتى رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله جبرئيل ـ وله ستمائة جناح على ساقه الدر ـ مثل القطر على البقل ـ قد ملأ ما بين السماء والأرض ـ وقال إذا أمر الله عز وجل ـ ميكائيل بالهبوط إلى الدنيا ـ صارت رجله في السماء السابعة ـ والأخرى في الأرض السابعة ، وإن لله ملائكة أنصافهم من برد وأنصافهم من نار ـ يقولون : يا مؤلفا بين البرد والنار ـ ثبت قلوبنا على طاعتك ـ.** — "Allah created the angels varied. Gabriel came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) having six hundred wings, with pearls upon his shanks like dew upon herbs, having filled what is between the heaven and the earth. He said: When Allah, Mighty and Majestic, commands Michael to descend to the world, one of his feet is in the seventh heaven and the other in the seventh earth. And indeed, Allah has angels whose halves are of hail and whose halves are of fire; they say: 'O You who have harmonized between hail and fire, make our hearts firm upon Your obedience.'"

He said: **إن لله ملكا بعد ما بين شحمة أذنه إلى عينه ـ مسيرة خمسمائة عام بخفقان الطير ـ.** — "Indeed, Allah has an angel, the distance between whose earlobe and his eye is a journey of five hundred years by the flight of a bird."

He said: **إن الملائكة لا يأكلون ولا يشربون ولا ينكحون ـ وإنما يعيشون بنسيم العرش ، وإن لله عز وجل ملائكة ركعا إلى يوم القيامة ـ وإن لله عز وجل ملائكة سجدا إلى يوم القيامة ـ.** — "Indeed, the angels do not eat, nor drink, nor marry; they only live by the breeze of the Throne. And indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, has angels who are in bowing (ركعا) until the Day of Resurrection, and indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, has angels who are in prostration (سجدا) until the Day of Resurrection."

Then Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said: **ما من شيء مما خلق الله عز وجل أكثر من الملائكة ـ وإنه ليهبط في كل يوم أو في كل ليلة ـ سبعون ألف ملك ، فيأتون البيت الحرام فيطوفون به ـ ثم يأتون رسول الله صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله ـ ثم يأتون أمير المؤمنين عليه‌السلام فيسلمون ـ ثم يأتون الحسين عليه‌السلام فيقيمون عنده ـ فإذا كان عند السحر وضع لهم معراج إلى السماء ـ ثم لا يعودون أبدا ـ.** — "There is nothing among what Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created more numerous than the angels. Indeed, every day or every night, seventy thousand angels descend; they come to the Sacred House (البيت الحرام) and circumambulate it, then they come to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny), then they come to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and offer salutations, then they come to al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and stay with him. When it is dawn, a ladder (معراج) to heaven is placed for them, and they never return."

Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: **إن الله عز وجل خلق إسرافيل وجبرائيل وميكائيل ـ من تسبيحة واحدة ، وجعل لهم السمع والبصر وجودة العقل وسرعة الفهم ـ.** — "Indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created Seraphiel, Gabriel, and Michael from a single glorification (تسبيحة), and He gave them hearing, sight, excellence of intellect, and speed of understanding."

The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said regarding the creation of the angels: **وملائكة خلقتهم وأسكنتهم سماواتك ـ فليس فيهم فترة ، ولا عندهم غفلة ، ولا فيهم معصية ، هم أعلم خلقك بك وأخوف خلقك منك ، وأقرب خلقك منك ، وأعملهم بطاعتك ، لا يغشاهم نوم العيون ولا سهو العقول ، ولا فترة الأبدان ـ لم يسكنوا الأصلاب ، ولم تضمهم الأرحام ، ولم تخلقهم من ماء مهين أنشأتهم إنشاء ـ فأسكنتهم سماواتك وأكرمتهم بجوارك ، وائتمنتهم على وحيك ، وجنبتهم الآفات ، ووقيتهم البليات ، وطهرتهم من الذنوب ، ولو لا قوتك لم يقووا ، ولو لا تثبيتك لم يثبتوا ، ولو لا رحمتك لم يطيعوا ، ولو لا أنت لم يكونوا ـ.** — "And angels whom You created and settled in Your heavens—there is no languor (فترة) in them, nor is there heedlessness (غفلة) with them, nor is there disobedience among them. They are the most knowledgeable of Your creation concerning You, the most fearful of Your creation of You, the nearest of Your creation to You, and the most diligent of them in Your obedience. Neither the sleep of eyes nor the forgetfulness of intellects nor the languor of bodies overcomes them. They did not inhabit loins, nor did wombs enclose them, nor did You create them from a base fluid. You originated them as a [special] creation, settled them in Your heavens, honored them with Your proximity, entrusted them with Your revelation, kept them away from afflictions, protected them from calamities, and purified them from sins. Were it not for Your power, they would not be powerful; were it not for Your making them firm, they would not be firm; were it not for Your mercy, they would not obey; and were it not for You, they would not be."

**أما إنهم على مكانتهم منك وطاعتهم إياك ـ ومنزلتهم عندك وقلة غفلتهم عن أمرك ـ لو عاينوا ما خفي عنهم منك لاحتقروا أعمالهم ، ولآزروا على أنفسهم ، ولعلموا أنهم لم يعبدوك حق عبادتك ـ سبحانك خالقا ومعبودا ـ ما أحسن بلاءك عند خلقك.** — "Verily, despite their station with You, their obedience to You, their rank before You, and their lack of heedlessness regarding Your command—had they witnessed what is hidden from them of You, they would have belittled their deeds, blamed themselves, and known that they have not worshipped You with the worship You truly deserve. Glory be to You as Creator and Worshipped; how excellent is Your trial (بلاءك) with Your creation."

In *al-Bihar*, from *al-Durr al-Manthur*, from Abu al-‘Ala’ ibn Sa‘d: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said one day to those sitting with him: **أطت السماء وحق لها أن تئط ـ ليس منها موضع قدم إلا عليه ملك راكع أو ساجد.** — "The heaven groaned, and it has every right to groan; there is not a foot's space within it except that there is an angel bowing or prostrating upon it." Then he recited: **وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الصَّافُّونَ وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْمُسَبِّحُونَ** "And indeed we are those who stand in ranks, and indeed we are those who celebrate [His] glory" (37:165-166).

From *al-Khisal*, with its chain of transmission from Muhammad ibn Talhah, reaching back to the Prophet (peace be upon him and his progeny), who said: **الملائكة على ثلاثة أجزاء فجزء لهم جناحان ـ وجزء لهم ثلاثة أجنحة وجزء لهم أربعة أجنحة.** — "The angels are of three parts: a part having two wings, a part having three wings, and a part having four wings."

I say: It was narrated in *al-Kafi* with its chain from ‘Abdullah ibn Talhah similarly. Perhaps what is intended is a description of the majority of angels, so that it does not contradict the context of the verse and other narrations.

From *al-Tawhid*, with its chain from Abu Hayyan al-Taymi from his father from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who said: **ليس أحد من الناس إلا ومعه ملائكة حفظة ـ يحفظونه من أن يتردى في بئر ـ أو يقع عليه حائط أو يصيبه سوء ـ فإذا حان أجله خلوا بينه وبين ما يصيبه ـ** — "There is no person among mankind but that with him are guardian angels (ملائكة حفظة) who protect him from falling into a well, or a wall falling upon him, or evil afflicting him. But when his appointed time (أجله) arrives, they leave him to what will afflict him..." (the narration).

From *al-Basa'ir*, from al-Sayyari from ‘Abdullah ibn Abi ‘Abdillah al-Farisi and others, reaching back to Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: **إن الكروبيين قوم من شيعتنا من الخلق الأول ـ جعلهم الله خلف العرش ـ لو قسم نور واحد منهم على أهل الأرض لكفاهم.** — "Indeed, the Cherubim (الكروبيون) are a group of our followers (شيعتنا) from the first creation; Allah placed them behind the Throne. If the light of one of them were divided among the people of the earth, it would suffice them." Then he said: "When Moses (peace be upon him) asked his Lord what he asked, He commanded one of the Cherubim, and he manifested himself to the mountain and turned it into dust."

From *al-Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah*, among his supplications for the Bearers of the Throne and every archangel: **اللهم وحملة عرشك الذين لا يفترون من تسبيحك ، ولا يسأمون من تقديسك ، ولا يستحسرون عن عبادتك ، ولا يؤثرون التقصير على الجد في أمرك ، ولا يغفلون عن الوله إليك ، وإسرافيل صاحب الصور الشاخص ـ الذي ينتظر منك الإذن وحلول الأمر ـ فينبه بالنفخة صرعى رهائن القبور ، وميكائيل ذو الجاه عندك والمكان الرفيع من طاعتك ـ وجبريل الأمين على وحيك ـ المطاع في سماواتك المكين لديك المقرب عندك ، والروح الذي هو على ملائكة الحجب ـ والروح الذي هو من أمرك ـ.** — "O Allah, and the Bearers of Your Throne, who never flag in glorifying You, nor tire of sanctifying You, nor weary of Your worship, nor prefer negligence over diligence in Your command, nor are heedless of their yearning for You. And Seraphiel, the master of the Trumpet, staring intently, awaiting Your permission and the arrival of the command, so that he may awaken with the blast those fallen as hostages of the graves. And Michael, the possessor of status with You and the high station through Your obedience. And Gabriel, the trustworthy over Your revelation, the one obeyed in Your heavens, the established with You, the near to You. And the Spirit (الروح) who is over the angels of the veils, and the Spirit who is of Your command."

**اللهم فصل عليهم وعلى الملائكة الذين من دونهم ـ من سكان سماواتك وأهل الأمانة على رسالاتك ، والذين لا يدخلهم سأمة من دءوب ـ ولا إعياء من لغوب ولا فتور ـ ولا تشغلهم عن تسبيحك الشهوات ـ ولا يقطعهم عن تعظيمك سهو الغفلات ، الخشع الأبصار فلا يرومون النظر إليك ، النواكس الأذقان الذين قد طالت رغبتهم فيما لديك ـ المستهترون بذكر آلائك ـ والمتواضعون دون عظمتك وجلال كبريائك ، والذين يقولون إذا نظروا إلى جهنم ـ تزفر على أهل معصيتك ـ سبحانك ما عبدناك حق عبادتك ـ.** — "O Allah, bless them and the angels below them—the inhabitants of Your heavens and the people of trust over Your messages; those whom no weariness from toil enters, nor exhaustion from fatigue, nor languor. Desires do not distract them from Your glorification, nor does the forgetfulness of heedlessness cut them off from Your magnification. Their eyes are humbled, so they do not seek to look at You; their chins are lowered, they whose longing for what is with You has been long. They are consumed with the remembrance of Your bounties and are humble before Your greatness and the majesty of Your pride. Those who say, when they look at Hell as it groans at the people of Your disobedience: 'Glory be to You, we have not worshipped You with the worship You truly deserve.'"

**فصل عليهم وعلى الروحانيين من ملائكتك ـ وأهل الزلفة عندك ـ وحمال الغيب إلى رسلك والمؤتمنين على وحيك ـ وقبائل الملائكة الذين اختصصتهم لنفسك ـ وأغنيتهم عن الطعام والشراب بتقديسك ـ وأسكنتهم بطون أطباق سماواتك ، والذين هم على أرجائها إذا نزل الأمر بتمام وعدك ـ.** — "So bless them and the spiritual ones (الروحانيين) among Your angels, the people of proximity to You, the carriers of the Unseen to Your messengers, the trustees over Your revelation, and the tribes of angels whom You have specialized for Yourself, and whom You have made independent of food and drink through Your sanctification, and whom You have settled within the layers of Your heavens, and those who will be upon its borders when the command descends for the fulfillment of Your promise."

**وخزان المطر وزواجر السحاب ـ والذي بصوت زجره يسمع زجل الرعود ، وإذا سبحت به حفيفة السحاب التمعت صواعق البروق ، ومشيعي الثلج والبرد ـ والهابطين مع قطر المطر إذا نزل ، والقوام على خزائن الرياح ، والموكلين بالجبال فلا تزول ، والذين عرفتهم مثاقيل المياه ـ وكيل ما يحويه لواعج الأمطار وعوالجها ـ ورسلك من الملائكة إلى أهل الأرض ـ بمكروه ما ينزل من البلاء ومحبوب الرخاء ـ.** — "And the treasurers of rain and the drivers of clouds—he by whose driving voice the rumble of thunders is heard, and when the rustling clouds are glorified by him, the bolts of lightning flash. And the escorts of snow and hail, and those who descend with the drops of rain when it falls. And those standing over the treasuries of the winds, and those entrusted with the mountains so they do not move. And those whom You have made to know the weights of the waters and the measure of what the torrential and surging rains contain. And Your messengers from the angels to the people of the earth with the disliked of descending affliction and the beloved of ease."

**والسفرة الكرام البررة والحفظة الكرام الكاتبين ، وملك الموت وأعوانه ، ومنكر ونكير ، ومبشر وبشير ، ورؤمان فتان القبور ، والطائفين بالبيت المعمور ، ومالك والخزنة ، ورضوان وسدنة الجنان ، والذين لا يعصون الله ما أمرهم ويفعلون ما يؤمرون ، والذين يقولون : سلام عليكم بما صبرتم فنعم عقبى الدار ، والزبانية الذين إذا قيل لهم : « خذوه فغلوه ثم الجحيم صلوه » ابتدروه سراعا ولم ينظروه ، ومن ألهمنا ذكره ولم نعلم مكانه منه وبأي أمر وكلته ، وسكان الهواء والأرض والماء ، ومن منهم على الخلق ـ.** — "And the noble and pious scribes (السفرة الكرام البررة), and the noble recording guardians (الحفظة الكرام الكاتبين), and the Angel of Death and his helpers, and Munkar and Nakir, and Mubashshir and Bashir, and Ruman the tester of graves, and those circumambulating the Frequented House (البيت المعمور), and Malik and the keepers [of Hell], and Ridwan and the attendants of the Gardens. And those who do not disobey Allah in what He commands them and do what they are commanded. And those who say: 'Peace be upon you for what you patiently endured; how excellent is the ultimate abode.' And the guards of Hell (الزبانية) who, when it is said to them: 'Seize him and fetter him, then cast him into Hell,' they rush to him quickly and do not grant him respite. And those whose mention You inspired us with but whose station with You we do not know, nor with what matter You entrusted them. And the inhabitants of the air, the earth, and the water, and those of them who are over the creation."

**فصل عليهم يوم تأتي كل نفس معها سائق وشهيد ـ وصل عليهم صلاة تزيدهم كرامة على كرامتهم ـ وطهارة على طهارتهم.** — "So bless them on the day when every soul comes, with it a driver and a witness. And bless them with a blessing that increases them in honor upon their honor and purity upon their purity." (The supplication).

In *al-Bihar*, from *al-Durr al-Manthur*, from Ibn Shihab: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) asked Gabriel to show himself to him in his [original] form. Gabriel said: "You will not be able to endure that." He said: "I would love that." So the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) went out to the prayer-place on a moonlit night, and Gabriel came to him in his form. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) fainted when he saw him. Then he regained consciousness while Gabriel was supporting him, placing one of his hands on his chest and the other between his shoulders. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said: **ما كنت أرى أن شيئا ممن يخلق هكذا** — "I did not think that anything among what is created was like this." Gabriel said: "How would it be if you saw Seraphiel? Indeed, he has twelve wings—a wing in the East and a wing in the West. The Throne is upon his shoulder, and at times he diminishes himself before the greatness of Allah until he becomes like a *was‘* (1), such that nothing carries His Throne except His greatness."

In *al-Safi*, from *al-Tawhid*, with its chain from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition, he said: Regarding His saying at the end of the verses: **ما زاغَ الْبَصَرُ وَما طَغى ـ لَقَدْ رَأى مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِ الْكُبْرى** "The eye did not swerve, nor did it transgress. Certainly he saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord" (53:17-18), he saw Gabriel in his form twice—this time and another time. That is because the creation of Gabriel is magnificent; he is among the spiritual ones (الروحانيين) whose creation and description none perceives except Allah.

From *al-Khisal*, with its chain from Muhammad ibn Marwan from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said: **إن جبرئيل أتاني فقال : إنا معشر الملائكة لا ندخل بيتا فيه كلب ـ ولا تمثال جسد ولا إناء يبال فيه.** — "Indeed, Gabriel came to me and said: 'We, the assembly of angels, do not enter a house in which there is a dog, nor a statue of a body, nor a vessel in which one urinates.'"

I say: There are other narrations regarding the description of the angels beyond counting, mentioned in the section on the Return (المعاد), the Ascension (المعراج) of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his progeny), and other miscellaneous sections. What we have cited is a sufficient sample of that.

In *al-‘Uyun*, in the chapter on what has come from al-Rida (peace be upon him) of collected reports, with his chain from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his progeny) said: **حسنوا القرآن بأصواتكم ـ فإن الصوت الحسن يزيد القرآن حسنا** — "Beautify the Quran with your voices, for indeed a beautiful voice increases the beauty of the Quran," and he recited: **يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ** "He adds to creation whatever He wishes" (35:1).

In *al-Tawhid*, with its chain from Zurarah from ‘Abdullah ibn Sulayman from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: I heard him saying: **إن القضاء والقدر خلقان من خلق الله ـ يزيد في الخلق ما يشاء.** — "Indeed, the Decree (القضاء) and the Destiny (القدر) are two creations among the creations of Allah; He adds to creation whatever He wishes."

In *al-Majma‘*, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ** "He adds to creation whatever He wishes" (35:1): Abu Hurayrah narrated from the Prophet (peace be upon him and his progeny) that he said: **هو الوجه الحسن والصوت الحسن والشعر الحسن.** — "It is the beautiful face, the beautiful voice, and the beautiful hair."

I say: The last three narrations are of the category of conceptual application (الجري والانطباق).

---
(1) With a *fathah* or *sukun* on the *sad*: a yellow bird smaller than a sparrow.

## (A Discourse on the Angels)

The mention of the angels is repeated in the Noble Quran, and none of them are mentioned by name except Gabriel (جبريل) and Michael (ميكال). Others are mentioned by description, such as the Angel of Death (ملك الموت), the Noble Scribes (الكرام الكاتبين), the Noble and Pious Scribes (السفرة الكرام البررة), the Watcher (الرقيب), the Ready Observer (العتيد), and others.

What Allah, the Glorified, has mentioned in His speech — and which the preceding traditions support — regarding their attributes and actions is:

First: They are honored beings who are intermediaries between Him, the Exalted, and the witnessed world. There is no event or occurrence, small or large, but that the angels have a role in it, and there is an angel entrusted with it or angels entrusted with it, according to the aspect or aspects within it. They have no role in this except to execute the divine command in its course or to establish it in its place, as the Exalted said: **لا يَسْبِقُونَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ وَهُمْ بِأَمْرِهِ يَعْمَلُونَ** "They do not outstrip Him in speech, and they act by His command" (21:27).

Second: They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them. They do not possess an independent selfhood with an independent will that desires something other than what Allah, the Glorified, desires. Thus, they do not act independently, nor do they change a matter Allah has charged them with through alteration, addition, or subtraction. The Exalted said: **لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ** "they do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they do what they are commanded" (66:6).

Third: The angels, despite their great number, are at different ranks of highness and lowness; some are above others and some are below others. Among them is the commander who is obeyed (آمر مطاع) and the commanded who obeys his command. The commander among them commands by the command of Allah, carrying it to the commanded, and the commanded is commanded by the command of Allah, obeying Him. They have nothing of themselves at all. The Exalted said: **وَما مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ مَقامٌ مَعْلُومٌ** "There is none among us but has a known station" (37:164), and He said: **مُطاعٍ ثَمَّ أَمِينٍ** "obeyed there and trustworthy" (81:21), and He said: **قالُوا ما ذا قالَ رَبُّكُمْ قالُوا الْحَقَّ** "They will say, ‘What did your Lord say?’ They will say, ‘The truth’" (34:23).

Fourth: They are never overcome, because they only act by the command and will of Allah. **وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ** "Nothing in the heavens or on the earth can frustrate Allah" (35:44). And Allah has said: **وَاللهُ غالِبٌ عَلى أَمْرِهِ** "and Allah is master of His affair" (12:21), and He said: **إِنَّ اللهَ بالِغُ أَمْرِهِ** "Indeed Allah carries through His command" (65:3).

From here it becomes clear that the angels are beings whose existence is sanctified from physical matter (المادة الجسمانية), which is subject to cessation, corruption, and change, and whose nature is gradual perfection (الاستكمال التدريجي) directed toward its goal—sometimes encountering obstacles and afflictions, thus being deprived of the goal and perishing before reaching it.

From here it also becomes clear that what has been reported in the narrations regarding the forms of the angels, their shapes, and their physical appearances — as a portion of which was presented in the preceding traditionary discussion — is only an exposition of their manifestations (تمثلاتهم) and appearances to those who described them among the prophets and Imams (peace be upon them). It is not a matter of [actual] formation (التصور) or shaping (التشكل). There is a difference between manifestation (التمثل) and shaping (التشكل). The manifestation of an angel as a human is its appearance to the one who witnesses it in the form of a human; thus, in the context of witnessing and perception, it possesses the form and shape of a human, while in itself and outside the context of perception, it is an angel possessing an angelic form. This is contrary to shaping and forming; for if it were to take the shape of a human and be formed in his form, it would become a human in itself, with no difference between the context of perception and what is outside it—it would be a human in reality (العين) and in the mind (الذهن) together. A discussion on the meaning of manifestation (التمثل) has already preceded in the commentary on Surah Maryam.

Allah, the Glorified, has confirmed the aforementioned meaning of manifestation in His saying regarding the story of the Messiah and Mary: **فَأَرْسَلْنا إِلَيْها رُوحَنا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَها بَشَراً سَوِيًّا** "Then We sent to her Our Spirit, and he manifested himself to her as a well-proportioned human" (19:17), and its commentary has already passed.

As for what is common on the tongues—that an angel is a subtle body (جسم لطيف) that can take different shapes except for the dog and the pig, and that the jinn is a subtle body that can take different shapes including the dog and the pig—it is something for which there is no proof from reason (عقل) nor from transmission (نقل) from the Book or the established Sunnah. As for what some have claimed regarding the consensus (إجماع) of the Muslims on this, in addition to it being contested, there is no proof for its authority (حجيته) in such doctrinal matters (المسائل الاعتقادية).
